mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.4pp, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that drives corporate revenue acceleration, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity that force rapid equity repricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of cases following a 0.5%+ drop in INDPRO, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stagnant or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy that erodes real corporate margins and compresses valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and non-falling FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising wage bills that support consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.015, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll increases across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product exceeding 0.2% confirms contractionary demand conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 falls by over 0.2%, or the signal predictive accuracy fails to exceed 53% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during peak employment cycles signals overheating supply constraints that trigger central bank tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when INDPRO rises during peak employment regimes, or the regime-conditional signal shows no statistical edge over a baseline model.
